title: Richly Spiced Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A nutty and silky Moroccan dish featuring chicken, branston pickle, and a blend of spices, cooked in a copper mug with a tandoor twist, this meal prep recipe is gluten free friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a copper mug over medium heat.
Add the chopped branston pickle and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken to the copper mug and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, ground cinnamon, ground ginger, salt, and black pepper to the copper mug and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the copper mug, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the copper mug from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
